title: Neomammillaria viperina
taxonomy: Neomammillaria viperina is a species of cactus in the genus Neomammillaria, which is part of the subfamily Cactoideae. This subfamily is within the family Cactaceae, commonly known as the cactus family. The Cactaceae family is part of the order Caryophyllales, which includes other flowering plants such as carnations and pinks. The Caryophyllales order is a part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the eudicots. The eudicots are a subgroup of the larger angiosperm clade, which includes all flowering plants.

intro: Neomammillaria viperina, also known as the "viper's tail" or "devil's tongue," is a type of cactus plant native to Mexico, characterized by its unique, tongue-shaped stems. Neomammillaria viperina has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Neomammillaria viperina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, sore throats, and other respiratory infections.
